articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: Sunday's papers lead on the fallout from the local elections and Gordon Brown's return to politics.
articles.title: Newspaper headlines: 'Plot to oust Starmer' and 'Gord help us all'

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: The BBC's Russia editor reports from a Red Square with none of the military hardware which the Kremlin normally showcases to project power internationally.
articles.title: Rosenberg: This year's Victory Day parade in Moscow felt very different

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: The Russian leader sees potential for Ukraine negotiations, but condemns the West's backing for President Zelensky.
articles.title: Putin says he thinks Ukraine conflict 'coming to an end'

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: Downing Street is trying to ignore the challenge from a former minister, but it has raised eyebrows around Westminster.
articles.title: Is Starmer's leadership under serious threat?

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: The new season of Tucci in Italy sees the actor travel the country finding out why there's a lot more to food than you might think.
articles.title: Stanley Tucci: We're losing our joy for food - it's time to bring it back

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: Catherine West tells the BBC if a leadership hopeful does not put themselves forward she will try to trigger a contest.
articles.title: Challenge Keir Starmer by Monday or I will, Labour MP Catherine West tells cabinet

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: BBC's Sarah Rainsford reports from the port in Tenerife where the MV Hondius is soon to dock, after a deadly hantavirus outbreak.
articles.title: Tenerife medics poised for arrival of virus-hit cruise ship

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: Fans are grappling with how to react to the withdrawal of Spain, Ireland and others over Israel's participation.
articles.title: Eurovision 2026 is here - but will the Israel boycott spoil the show?

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: The first-past-the-post system favoured Reform in this English local elections
articles.title: How the winner-takes-all voting system has turned on Labour and the Tories

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: The National Education Union says it will hold a formal ballot this autumn without "urgent action".
articles.title: Teachers in England move towards striking over pay
